behavior_in_task_description.reason: Tests assert specific implementation details not fully specified in instruction.md: test_source_files_contain_new_functions checks for exact names 'detectCatalog', 'updateCatalogVersion', 'CatalogInfo'; test_get_latest_version_resolves_dist_tags checks for exact string 'tags[to]'; test_yaml_dependency_added checks yaml version starts with '2.'; test_npm_pkg_has_update_catalog_test checks for existence of '__tests__/update-catalog.test.ts'. The instruction mentions creating update-catalog.ts but

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction describes what's broken and what needs to be fixed at a functional level (detect catalogs, update catalog files, return install command, resolve dist-tags) without specifying exact code changes, line numbers, or specific implementation details like function signatures.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert gold-specific implementation details: exact function names 'detectCatalog' and 'updateCatalogVersion', exact type name 'CatalogInfo', exact expression 'tags[to]' in get-latest-version.ts, exact method name 'getInstallCommand'. An alternative correct fix using different names (e.g., 'findCatalog', 'writeCatalogVersion', 'CatalogDetails', 'tags.get(to)') would fail these tests despite being functionally correct.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Almost every test is purely text-matching: test_source_files_contain_new_functions does 'assert "detectCatalog" in content', test_get_turbo_upgrade_command_handles_catalog_info checks 'assert "catalogInfo" in content', test_get_latest_version_resolves_dist_tags checks 'assert "tags[to]" in content', etc. The only behavioral tests are test_repo_typescript_builds (which runs build/check-types) and test_repo_lint. No test actually imports/calls the code or verifies catalog update behavior.

test_not_tautological.reason: A stub update-catalog.ts containing only comments with the strings 'detectCatalog', 'updateCatalogVersion', 'CatalogInfo', 'catalog:', and 'yaml' would pass test_source_files_contain_new_functions and test_update_catalog_ts_imports_yaml without implementing any actual functionality. Similarly, adding 'catalogInfo' as a comment to get-turbo-upgrade-command.ts passes test_get_turbo_upgrade_command_handles_catalog_info.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution is in /workspace/turborepo accessible to the agent via solve.sh which curls the exact GitHub PR patch. The agent could simply run 'curl -sL https://patch-diff.githubusercontent.com/raw/vercel/turborepo/pull/12496.patch | git apply' to get the solution. Additionally, since tests are purely string-matching, the agent could grep test files or solution files to discover exact expected strings.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Instruction requires 4 behaviors: detect catalog references, update catalog file with version prefix preservation, return install command for catalogs, resolve dist-tags. None of these behaviors are actually tested functionally—tests only check for string presence in source files, not that catalog files are correctly updated or that install commands are returned.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-named with descriptive function names and docstrings, grouped into standalone functions and a TestRepoTests class. Each test clearly indicates what it's checking.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task doesn't produce structured data output like JSON/CSV/API/DB; it modifies source code files.

no_typos.reason: File paths, commands, and variable names in instruction.md appear correct and consistent with the test file references.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction explicitly names the exact files to modify (index.ts, get-turbo-upgrade-command.ts, get-latest-version.ts) and specifies creating a new file (update-catalog.ts) at a specific path, plus details about what each file needs. This goes beyond describing the bug to prescribing the solution architecture.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code-only task, not an agent config edit task.
